epigraph.text: I will make justice the line, and righteousness the plumb line.

content.title: Perpendicular Lines
content.content:
Two lines are **perpendicular** if they 
intersect to form right angles. We write $\ell \perp m$.

content.title: Perpendicular Bisector
content.content:
The **perpendicular bisector** of a 
segment is a line that is perpendicular to the segment at its midpoint.

content.title: Perpendicular Transversal Theorem
content.content:
If a transversal is perpendicular to one of two parallel lines, it is 
perpendicular to the other.
content.label: Theorem

content.content:
Let $\ell \parallel m$ and let transversal $t \perp \ell$.

Since $t \perp \ell$, the angle at the intersection is $90°$.

This angle and its corresponding angle at line $m$ are congruent 
(Corresponding Angles Postulate).

Therefore, $t$ meets $m$ at a $90°$ angle, so $t \perp m$.

content.title: Lines Perpendicular to a Transversal Theorem
content.content:
In a plane, if two lines are perpendicular to the same line, then they 
are parallel to each other.
content.label: Theorem

content.content:
Let $\ell \perp t$ and $m \perp t$.

Both create $90°$ angles with $t$. These are corresponding angles, and 
they are congruent (both are $90°$).

By the Converse of the Corresponding Angles Postulate, $\ell \parallel m$.

content.title: Perpendicular Bisector Theorem
content.content:
If a point is on the perpendicular bisector of a segment, then it is 
equidistant from the endpoints of the segment.
content.label: Theorem

content.content:
Let $\ell$ be the perpendicular bisector of $\overline{AB}$, meeting 
$\overline{AB}$ at midpoint $M$. Let $P$ be any point on $\ell$.

In triangles $\triangle PMA$ and $\triangle PMB$:

- $PM = PM$ (reflexive)

- $AM = MB$ (M is midpoint)

- $\angle PMA = \angle PMB = 90°$ ($\ell \perp \overline{AB}$)

By SAS (which we'll prove in Chapter 4), $\triangle PMA \cong \triangle PMB$.

Therefore, $PA = PB$.

content.title: Converse of Perpendicular Bisector Theorem
content.content:
If a point is equidistant from the endpoints of a segment, then it is 
on the perpendicular bisector of the segment.
content.label: Theorem

content.text: Distance from a Point to a Line

content.title: Distance from Point to Line
content.content:
The **distance from a point to a line** 
is the length of the perpendicular segment from the point to the line.

content.text: This is the shortest distance from the point to any point on the line.

marginNotes.text:
The Lord was standing beside a wall built 
with a plumb line, with a plumb line in his hand.
